warnings: This sheet (A-09) presents door types D1-D3 across the top row and window type(s) below/beside them — laid out as 4 columns top (D1, D2, D3, W1) then 2 further window rows below the D1 column (W2, and a bottom hexagon-3 labeled 'บานกระทุ้ง' row here called W3_บานกระทุ้ง). D/W numbering re-derived the same way house 1's schedule was (raw sheet reuses independently-restarting numbering per door vs window that would collide if used verbatim as element_id).

W2 and W3_บานกระทุ้ง may be the SAME window type transcribed from two different callout instances on the sheet (both 'บานกระทุ้ง', both aluminum/6mm glass) rather than two genuinely distinct window designs — flagged as a real open question for human review rather than merged or guessed, since W2 has full dimensions (1.40 x 2.50, EQ+EQ split, height break 2.00+0.50) while the bottom hexagon-3 row has no dimension printed in its table cell at all.

Content (door/window construction specs — aluminum frame/6mm glass/white powder coat, wood door 2x4 frame, PVC bathroom door) is consistent with house 1's material list (A-01/A-02) which specifies the same aluminum/glass standards (มอก. 284-2530) and hardware standards program-wide.

Cross-reference: elevation sheets (A-06/A-07, หน้า08/09) show window heights 2.15m and 2.45m at some openings that do NOT exactly match any single D/W schedule entry width/height pair recorded here (W1=1.40x2.05, W2=1.40x2.50) -- flagged as an unresolved cross-sheet dimension mismatch for human review, possibly because those elevation heights are measured to a different reference line (sill-to-head vs full rough-opening) or correspond to a door/opening not itemized as a separate schedule row on this sheet (e.g. the large X-hatched carport-side opening).
